entries.summary: Restraining the respondents from removal and/or cutting, damaging of Gillbert Hill in any manner situated at Andheri (West).
No one appears for the Petitioner. Appointed Mr.Shiraj Rustumji as an Amicus curie in this matter.
The Counsel for the petitioner prays for time for filing rejoinder to the reply Affidavit filed by the respondents.

It is stated that small portion admeasuring about 715 sq.mtrs. of the Gilbert hill is situate on the northern side of the property. Request the Director, Indian Institute of Technology, Powai to nominate an expert from the institute to inspect the Gilbert hill, Andheri (West) CTS Nos.254, 276, 277, 105, 108, 210, 211, 252, 250 and 216 and report this to court.

Registry of the court is directed to send a copy of the writ petition to the Director, Indian Institute of Technology, Powai with a request to nominate an expert from the institute to inspect the Gilbert Hill, Andheri (West).
highlights: Director, Indian Institute of Technology, Powai

entries.summary: Registrar, Indian Institute of Technology, Powai was unable to nominate any faculty member. So, request Mr. B.A. Desai, Additional Solicitor General of India as to whether Geological Survey of India or National Environmental Engineering Research Institute can nominate an expert for his opinion to the aspects noticed above.

Appoint the Committee of Dr. S. Y. Mhaiskar, Professor in Civil Engineering and Principal, Sardar Patel College of Engineering, and Mr. Cyrus R. Tata, Senior Civil and Soil Engineer, to inspect Gilbert Hill, Andheri (West), and report to this Court.
Report of the Committee is still awaited.
Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) and the Municipal Corporation will file their replies on Construction matter.
Report is received from the Engineers appointed who informed the Court about the strength of the hill.

Counsel urged that the Court should pass interim order staying all developmental activities of all nature and kind within the radius of 100 meters from the foot hills and in the periphery of the Gilbert Hill.
One week's time is granted to parties to carry out the amendment and complete their pleadings/reply affidavits within the time granted.

The court directed that no development activities would be carried on the land bearing CTS No.250, 251 and 254 of Andheri, Mumbai as probably in September 1952, Gilbert hill was declared as a National Park.
Calling for the respondents to state whether in 1952 the hill was declared as a National Park or not.
Counsel appearing for the Corporation states that the Corporation would not permit any development activities except for recreation ground which shall be used for public purpose.
Counsel appearing for the Petitioner to file an affidavit including the mentioning of C.T.S. numbers in relation to which the Gilbert Hill has been removed for the development.

The Learned A.G.P. on seeking instructions from Mr. A.G. Girkar, Secretary, Urban Development, makes a statement that the State Government could take a decision on the proposal submitted by the B.M.C. in respect of declaring Gilbert Hill in Heritage Grade-II.
Notification sent to learned counsel of the Union of India to file an affidavit of the concerned Officer stating therein whether the site, has ever been declared as a National Park or not.

Maharashtra Government has declared Gilbert Hill as a heritage site under the Development Control Regulations, 1991 and as a consequence of this Notification, it is submitted by the learned counsel for the respondents that no building activity would be permissible there.
The learned counsel for the respective respondents pray for time to seek instructions in regard to disposal of praecipe.
Petition is disposed of. Stand over.
